mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding aggregate income and consumption demand, which drives higher expected corporate revenues and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns following positive PAYEMS momentum are negative on average over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate crosses above 1.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy transmits rapidly into higher discount rates and tighter financial conditions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive average 20-day returns in more than half of all 3-month windows where FEDFUNDS exceeds 1.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in consumer price inflation and labor market slack creates a stagflationary profit squeeze and valuation de-rating; the joint elevation of CPIAUCSL and UNRATE produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days that neither series generates in isolation.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and UNRATE yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or UNRATE alone explains the return variation without CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month growth in industrial production is interpreted by markets as evidence of overheating that warrants additional rate hikes, reversing the typical expansionary signal and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified tightening cycles, positive INDPRO momentum coincides with positive average S&P 500 20-day returns over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in retail and food services sales signals weakening near-term consumer demand and falling corporate top-line growth, which drags S&P 500 returns lower over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month declines in RSAFS are followed by positive S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1400 thousand units indicates a slump in residential investment and construction employment that spills over into broad economic activity, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive on average during all months when HOUST is below 1400 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals a turning point in the labor market that abruptly reprices earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are non-negative in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE has risen more than 0.3 percentage points over the prior 3 months during a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real chained GDP reflects the net expansion of domestic output and income; higher prints drive upward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarterly growth in GDPC1 coincides with negative average S&P 500 60-day returns over any 3-year rolling window.
